            const landStart = Math.max(landStartTime[i], waterEnd);
            const option2End = landStart + landDuration[i];
            
            const currentMin = Math.min(option1End, option2End);
            if (currentMin < minTime) {
                minTime = currentMin;
            }
        }
    }
    
    return minTime;
}

Then for each i and j, compute the two options and take the min between them, then compare to the overall min.

So the code would be:

function solve(landStartTime, landDuration, waterStartTime, waterDuration) {

    let minTime = Infinity;

    const n = landStartTime.length;

    const m = waterStartTime.length;

    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {

        for (let j = 0; j < m; j++) {

            // Option 1: land first, then water

            const landEnd = landStartTime[i] + landDuration[i];

            const waterStart = Math.max(waterStartTime[j], landEnd);

            const option1End = waterStart + waterDuration[j];

            // Option 2: water first, then land

            const waterEnd = waterStartTime[j] + waterDuration[j];

            const landStart = Math.max(landStartTime[i], waterEnd);

            const option2End = landStart + landDuration[i];

            const currentMin = Math.min(option1End, option2End);

            if (currentMin < minTime) {

                minTime = currentMin;

            }

        }

    }

    return minTime;

}
